title: Tasks & Kanban
lede: Never let a follow-up slip through the cracks again. iMash Tasks gives your team a shared to-do list with priorities, due dates, checklists, time tracking, and comments — plus a drag-and-drop Project Kanban that shows your entire pipeline at a glance.

steps.title: Open the Tasks list
steps.content:
1. In the top navigation, click CRM, then click the Tasks tab (or go directly to /crm/tasks). The counter on the tab shows your total number of tasks.
  2. Every task appears in a searchable table. The columns are:
  • Task — the title (click it to open the task) plus a short description preview. A red clock icon means the task is overdue.
  • Status — TODO, IN PROGRESS, BLOCKED, REVIEW, COMPLETED, or CANCELLED.
  • Priority — LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, or URGENT.
  • Assigned To — the team member responsible (or Unassigned).
  • Progress — a percentage bar that fills automatically as checklist items get checked off.
  • Comments — how many comments the task has.
  • Active — shows Running · X min when you have a live work timer on that task.
  • Actions — View, Edit, Copy, and Delete buttons for each row.

images.caption: The Tasks list — status, priority, assignee, progress, and comments for every task
The Tasks list — status, priority, assignee, progress, and comments for every task

steps.title: Create a task
steps.content:
1. Click + Add Task in the top-right of the list.
  2. Fill in the form:
  • Task Title (required) — a short, action-oriented name like "Call back Sarah about the quote".
  • Description — details, context, links. Click Enhance Description and the built-in AI rewrites your rough notes into a clear, polished description.
  • Priority — Low, Medium, High, or Urgent (defaults to Medium).
  • Status — To Do, In Progress, Review, or Blocked (defaults to To Do).
  • Assign To — pick the team member who owns this task.
  • Category — Follow Up, Meeting, Call, Email, Research, Development, Review, or Other. Handy for filtering later.
  • Start Date and Due Date — tasks past their due date are automatically flagged OVERDUE.
  • Estimated Hours — your time budget; compared against actual tracked time.
  • Tags — free-form labels to group related tasks.

images.caption: The Create New Task form — title, AI-enhanced description, priority, status, dates, and tags
The Create New Task form — title, AI-enhanced description, priority, status, dates, and tags

steps.title: Add a checklist and link the task to a CRM record
steps.content:
Scroll down in the same form for two power features:

  1. Checklist — break the task into sub-steps:
    • Click Add Checklist Item and type each step (mark any as Done already, or Remove it).
    • Or click Generate with AI — iMash reads your title and description and drafts the checklist for you.
    • The task's Progress percentage is calculated automatically from completed checklist items.
  2. Related To — attach the task to the record it's about:
    • Related To Type — Lead, Contact, Account, Campaign, Call, or Opportunity.
    • Related Entity — search and pick the exact record.
    • On the task page you'll get a Related card with a one-click View Details jump to that lead, contact, account, campaign, or call.
  3. Click Create Task to save (or Cancel to discard).
💡 Linking a task to a lead or call keeps the full story in one place — anyone picking up the task sees exactly who it's about without hunting through the CRM.

images.alt: Checklist and Related To sections of the task form
images.caption: Checklist items (with AI generation) and the Related To link at the bottom of the form

images.alt: Checklist and Related To sections of the task form
images.caption: Checklist items (with AI generation) and the Related To link at the bottom of the form

steps.title: Search, filter, and sort the list
steps.content:
1. Type in the Search tasks... box to match against task titles and descriptions.
  2. Open the Filter By dropdown and pick one or more filter types — a dedicated selector appears for each:
  • Status — show only certain statuses (multi-select).
  • Priority — e.g. only HIGH and URGENT (multi-select).
  • Assigned — Assigned to Me, Unassigned, or a specific team member.
  • Due Date — Overdue, Due Today, or Due This Week.
  • Category — filter by the task category.
  • Owner — filter by which account (main or sub-account) owns the task.
💡 Start every morning with Filter By → Assigned → Assigned to Me plus Due Date → Overdue — that's your instant "what's on fire" view.

images.caption: The toolbar: search, Filter By, view switcher, and Add Task
The toolbar: search, Filter By, view switcher, and Add Task
Filter By offers Status, Priority, Assigned, Due Date, Category, and Owner filters
Filter By offers Status, Priority, Assigned, Due Date, Category, and Owner filters

steps.title: Switch to Board view
steps.content:
1. In the toolbar, change the view selector from Table View to Board View.
  2. Your tasks regroup into five status columns: TODO, IN PROGRESS, REVIEW, BLOCKED, and COMPLETED, each with a live count.
  3. Every card shows the priority tag, an overdue clock icon when relevant, the checklist progress bar, the assignee's avatar, and how soon it's due.
  4. Click any card to open the full task page.
💡 Board view is perfect for stand-ups — one glance tells you what's stuck in BLOCKED and what's waiting in REVIEW. To move a task to another column, open it and change its status (see the task page below).

images.caption: Board View groups tasks into TODO, IN PROGRESS, REVIEW, BLOCKED, and COMPLETED columns
Board View groups tasks into TODO, IN PROGRESS, REVIEW, BLOCKED, and COMPLETED columns

steps.title: Open a task and track your time
steps.content:
1. Click a task's title (or the green eye View button in the Actions column) to open its page.
  2. The header shows the title, status / priority / OVERDUE tags, any tags you added, the description, and the checklist progress bar.
  3. Click Start Timer to begin tracking work time — a live Timer Running stopwatch appears in the header, and the Tasks list shows Running · X min in the Active column.
  4. Click Stop Timer when you're done. The session is saved to the Time Logs tab and added to the task's total Time Spent.
  5. Click Edit Task to change any field from the create form.

images.caption: The task page — header with Start Timer and Edit Task, checklist tab, and the sidebar cards
The task page — header with Start Timer and Edit Task, checklist tab, and the sidebar cards

steps.title: Work the tabs: Checklist, Comments, Activity, Files, Time Logs
steps.content:
The main panel of the task page has five tabs:

  • Checklist — tick items off as you complete them; the progress bar updates instantly. Each item has its own 💬 comment thread and 📄 file attachments, so evidence lives right next to the step it belongs to.
  • Comments — type in Add a comment... and click Add Comment. You can edit or delete your own comments later.
  • Activity — an automatic history of everything: status changes, timer starts, checklist toggles, comments.
  • Files — upload documents to the task itself, and see which checklist items have files attached.
  • Time Logs — every timer session with who worked, when, and for how long.

images.caption: The Comments tab — add, edit, and delete comments; the other tabs cover checklist, activity, files, and time logs
The Comments tab — add, edit, and delete comments; the other tabs cover checklist, activity, files, and time logs

steps.title: Update status and read the sidebar
steps.content:
The right-hand column of the task page keeps the vitals in view:

  • Status & Actions — a dropdown to move the task through 📋 To Do → 🚀 In Progress → 👀 Review → 🚫 Blocked → ✅ Completed → ❌ Cancelled. Changing it here updates the list and board immediately.
  • Task Details — Progress %, Assignee, Due Date (red if overdue), Time Spent vs. estimate, Owner, Created and Updated dates, and Category.
  • Related — if the task is linked to a lead, contact, account, campaign, or call, this card names it and the View Details button jumps straight to it.

images.caption: The Task Details card — progress, assignee, due date, time spent, and ownership at a glance
The Task Details card — progress, assignee, due date, time spent, and ownership at a glance

steps.title: Copy, delete, and bulk actions
steps.content:
• Copy (the gray icon in the Actions column) duplicates a task as a fresh To Do — same title, description, checklist (all unchecked), and assignee, with progress and time logs reset. Perfect for recurring work like weekly reports.
  • Delete (the red icon) removes a task after a confirmation prompt.
  • Bulk delete — tick the checkboxes on multiple rows and a Delete Selected (N) button appears in the toolbar.
⚠️ Deleting is meant for tasks created by mistake. For finished work, set the status to Completed instead — you keep the history, time logs, and reporting trail.

images.alt: Row action buttons: View, Edit, Copy, Delete
images.caption: The Actions column on every row — View (eye), Edit (pencil), Copy, and Delete

images.alt: Row action buttons: View, Edit, Copy, Delete
images.caption: The Actions column on every row — View (eye), Edit (pencil), Copy, and Delete

steps.title: The Project Kanban: your pipeline on one board
steps.content:
Tasks aren't the only board in town. The Project Kanban turns your entire lead-and-contact pipeline into a drag-and-drop board.

  1. In the top navigation, click CRM, then click the Kanban tab at the far right of the tab strip (or go directly to /crm/kanban).
  2. Each column is one of your CRM lifecycle stages (New, Contacted, No Answer, Voicemail, and any custom stages you've defined in CRM Settings), with a badge showing the total and separate lead / contact counts.
  3. Every card is a lead (blue avatar) or contact (green avatar) with its creation date.
  4. Drag a card to another column to change that person's lifecycle stage — the CRM record updates instantly.
  5. Click a card's name to open the full lead or contact details in a side panel.
  6. Columns load more cards automatically as you scroll down.
💡 The columns come from your stage configuration in CRM Settings — rename, recolor, or add stages there and the board follows.

images.caption: The Project Kanban — leads and contacts grouped by lifecycle stage; drag cards to move them between stages
The Project Kanban — leads and contacts grouped by lifecycle stage; drag cards to move them between stages

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaDescription: Create and assign tasks with priorities, due dates, AI-generated checklists, comments, files, and time tracking in iMash — plus the drag-and-drop Project Kanban for your lead and contact pipeline.
